authoritative_sources.title: Semantic Versioning 2.0.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SemVer is the public specification for major, minor, patch, pre-release, build metadata, and compatibility semantics used by many software release processes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: version decision and compatibility impact review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or when SemVer publishes a new specification or materially changes guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: Git Basics - Tagging
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Git SCM publishes official Git documentation for tags, annotated tags, signed tags, tag push behavior, and tag deletion behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: branch, tag, and release-point evidence review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Git release documentation changes tag or signing behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: About releases
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official documentation for repository release objects, tags, release notes, assets, and project release communication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: release publication, release notes, and asset delivery
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes release features, release notes behavior, or asset handling.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: Deployment environments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub defines deployment environment gates, protection rules, required reviewers, branch restrictions, and environment-scoped secrets used as release controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hold/release boundary and deployment approval review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes deployment environment behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Using artifact attestations to establish provenance for builds
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official guidance for creating and verifying artifact and SBOM attestations from GitHub Actions release workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: artifact provenance and SBOM evidence review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes attest action versions, attestation permissions, or verification behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Secure use reference for GitHub Actions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official security guidance for safe Actions usage, secrets, untrusted input, third-party actions, token permissions, and runner trust.
practical_use_in_pack: third-party action and token risk review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes Actions security guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: Releases
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitLab publishes official documentation for release entries, tags, assets, milestones, evidence, links, and release workflow behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: GitLab release publication and evidence adaptation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on GitLab major/minor release behavior changes.

authoritative_sources.title: Protected environments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitLab publishes the official protected-environment model used to restrict deployments to approved users, groups, roles, or rules.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitLab changes protected environment behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Merge request approvals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitLab publishes official merge request approval behavior used to distinguish review evidence from final approval authority.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitLab changes merge request approval behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Jenkins Pipeline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Jenkins Project publishes the official Pipeline handbook for Pipeline as code, Jenkinsfile source control, stages, steps, continuous delivery, and audit trail.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Jenkins release workflow inspection and evidence review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Jenkins Pipeline documentation or LTS behavior materially changes.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218: Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is a U.S. national secure software development framework covering security practices, evidence, vulnerability response, and secure release controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: secure release control and remediation review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on NIST revision or new federal secure software guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP CI/CD Security Cheat Sheet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP publishes recognized public guidance specifically for CI/CD risks, secure configuration, IAM, secrets, third-party code, integrity, and visibility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: release security review and failure-mode handling
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on OWASP cheat sheet or CI/CD risk taxonomy update.

authoritative_sources.title: SLSA v1.2 Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SLSA is an industry-consensus framework for software supply-chain security, build integrity, provenance, distribution, and verification roles.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: provenance and artifact integrity gate review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when SLSA publishes a new specification version.

authoritative_sources.title: CycloneDX Specification Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CycloneDX is a public SBOM and supply-chain transparency specification used for dependency and evidence artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SBOM evidence and release package review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when CycloneDX publishes a new specification version.

authoritative_sources.title: Open Container Initiative Image Format Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OCI defines the container image manifest, layers, descriptors, configuration, and content-addressable identity used in common release artifact flows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: container artifact and immutable promotion review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when OCI image-spec releases or main specification changes materially.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Docs: About pull request reviews
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official pull request review behavior used to separate review evidence, requested changes, approvals, and final merge authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review evidence, approval boundary, and resubmission tracking
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes pull request review behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM is a major professional computing society and its ethics code covers quality, privacy, confidentiality, harm avoidance, truthful claims, and professional judgment boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality, client delivery, and professional judgment boundary
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM code revision.

authoritative_sources.title: Current package task baseline from release-engineering-specialist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from release-engineering-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Repository-committed baseline used by issue #1625 to preserve the specialist's high-value release engineering boundary while refreshing stale build-era assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: historical scope comparison and minimum rigor baseline
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when the package is refreshed again or when legacy baseline assumptions are superseded by new issue requirements.

workflow_stages.name: engagement and release evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm release request type, change scope, repository and branch state, target version, release calendar, target environments, artifact inventory, tenant facts, systems of record, confidentiality terms, approval matrix, source freshness, and expected output shape before assessing release readiness.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: release artifact and control review
workflow_stages.description: Inspect release branches, tags, versions, changelog inputs, release notes, build outputs, package metadata, artifact digests, SBOMs, signatures, attestations, provenance records, tests, scans, environment gates, and deployment approvals against release correctness, maintainability, security, artifact integrity, and release-safety criteria.
source_classes: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
Public security and software quality guidance
Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability

workflow_stages.name: quality, security, and release disposition
workflow_stages.description: Classify release evidence as satisfied, failed, unclear, blocked, hold, or release-ready recommendation without granting final release, contractual, audit, or client approval authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Public security and software quality guidance

workflow_stages.name: defect, incident, rollback, and corrective-action handling
workflow_stages.description: Convert failed release runs, recurring defects, blocked gates, deployment regressions, artifact gaps, rollback readiness gaps, and nonconformances into incident summaries, remediation plans, rerun criteria, rollback recommendations, and escalation packets.
source_classes: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
Public security and software quality guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: delivery, confidentiality, commercialization, and signoff gating
workflow_stages.description: Before packaging release notes, deployment notes, audit evidence, or commercialization review outputs, check recipient authorization, confidentiality, tenant policy, engagement scope, client commitments, contractual commitments, commercialization review, approval authority, and professional judgment boundaries.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: request, acceptance criteria, engagement scope, release scope, confidentiality terms, client commitments, tenant policy, release calendar, freeze window, and approval matrix
source repository, pull request or merge request, release branch, protected branch setting, tag policy, signed or annotated tag record, release object, release asset, and package metadata
change request, issue ticket, defect ticket, nonconformance record, incident record, remediation tracker, release train plan, rollout matrix, and release checklist
workflow run IDs, job logs, test reports, scan outputs, SBOMs, artifact digests, container images, package artifacts, signatures, provenance records, attestations, checksums, and release notes inputs

protected environment approvals, merge or pull request reviews, deployment records, rollback plan, health checks, observability dashboards, post-release verification records, reviewer responses, and approval records

release process recommendation, versioning and tagging recommendation, gating criteria and rollback plan, deployment note, quality or safety review note, control or incident summary, corrective-action escalation, evidence checklist, and confidentiality-safe delivery note
systems_of_record: source repository and pull request or merge request system
release management or change management system, issue tracker, defect tracker, incident tracker, remediation tracker, and approval register
CI/CD platform record for GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or tenant-approved equivalent

workflow run history, job logs, test report store, scan report store, package registry, container registry, artifact registry, release evidence store, SBOM store, signing key or identity records, attestation or provenance registry, and release notes store

environment protection settings, protected environment approvals, deployment history, rollback records, health checks, observability dashboards, logs, metrics, traces, alerts, and SLO or release success dashboards
tenant document management system, engagement scope, confidentiality records, client commitments, commercialization review records, and approval matrix

allowed: recommend release process designs, release train stages, branch and tag conventions, versioning decisions, changelog structure, artifact evidence gates, rollout checks, rollback criteria, and review findings
classify release evidence as satisfied, failed, unclear, blocked, hold, or release-ready recommendation for review support
request missing artifacts and return low-confidence cases to orchestrator
route adjacent or authority-bound work to the correct owner
escalate: release approval, merge approval, contractual acceptance, legal/compliance interpretation, audit attestation, client rights, commercialization approval, or licensed/professional judgment is required
tenant policy conflicts with public source guidance or confidentiality terms change the allowed output
critical repository, branch, tag, artifact, release, deployment, incident, approval, or evidence records are missing or contradictory

security risk acceptance, enterprise IAM, SOC implementation, platform operations, runner fleet provisioning, SRE incident command, production rollback execution, or network/cloud operations are required
refuse: requests for production infrastructure provisioning, capacity management, platform tuning, privileged deployment execution, or live incident operations
requests for product roadmap, pricing, sales enablement, launch strategy, staffing, or go-to-market business decisions
requests for security architecture outside release-time controls, IAM program design, SOC implementation, legal/compliance interpretation, attestation, commercialization approval, or final signoff
requests to bypass protected environments, manual approvals, required reviews, quality gates, artifact integrity checks, release holds, or documented safety controls without explicit authority
requests to expose confidential tenant/client release notes, logs, scan reports, incident notes, package evidence, or delivery records to unapproved recipients

domain_failure_modes: generic release advice that is not tied to provided change records, branches, tags, build artifacts, package metadata, run IDs, logs, test reports, scan outputs, deployment records, approvals, incidents, or tenant systems of record
unsupported clearance of a release hold, quality gate, audit package, review finding, or defect without remediation, rerun, artifact, and approval evidence
versioning or tag recommendations that ignore compatibility impact, release history, annotated or signed tag policy, or package metadata
artifact integrity, SBOM, provenance, signing, digest, or package evidence treated as present when source records are missing
release recommendations that assume privileged signing, registry, deployment, infrastructure, or production access not supplied by tenant authority
over-authoritative legal, compliance, security-risk, attestation, contractual, client acceptance, commercialization, or final release approval claims
confidential client or tenant release notes drafts, logs, scan reports, incident notes, package evidence, or delivery records sent to an unapproved recipient or summarized beyond approved scope
security guidance that turns into enterprise security architecture, IAM program design, SOC implementation, or risk acceptance
platform operations, SRE incident command, product strategy, sales enablement, staffing, or application implementation work retained instead of routed
tenant-specific policy conflicts confidently resolved without approval owner review

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing evidence or workpaper support
Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
normal implementation/design work
maintainability and correctness review
edge cases and failure modes
security and quality expectations where applicable
out-of-scope rejection
low-confidence escalation
no unreviewed regressions
Inspection, review, or control verification.
Defect or incident handling.
Disposition or hold recommendation.
Corrective-action escalation.
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness.
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action.
Insufficient evidence to clear a hold or close a finding.
Repeat defect or recurring incident.
Request to bypass a documented safety or quality control.
Scenario proving client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness.
Scenario proving escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.
Scenario proving output fidelity for: release process recommendations
Scenario proving output fidelity for: versioning and tagging recommendations
Scenario proving output fidelity for: gating criteria and rollback plans
Scenario proving output fidelity for: deployment notes for packaging and commercialization review

unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack uses public Git, GitHub, GitLab, Jenkins, security, artifact, and professional sources and records that tenant-supplied platform versions, policies, release calendars, and approval rules must be retrieved before stack-specific claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Legal, compliance, attestation, contractual, commercialization, client acceptance, and release approval authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack refuses final signoff and routes authority-bound questions to human owners or orchestrator.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Production execution and incident command authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack provides release guidance and handoff evidence only; production operation, rollback execution, runner provisioning, and SRE incident control route out of lane.
